seed42.Time is:
a dimension, and the fourth dimension of space-time
The theory that time exists as another dimension to which we move in relation with all things. This idea was introduced by Albert Einstein.
Einstein stated that not only does our perception exist within this 4-dimensional spacetime continuum but it also simultaneously influences how events are perceived. Events have an order; they happen at certain times relative to each other. Time thus has the properties of directionality (the sense from past into future) as well as ordering or simultaneity between events.
For example, if event A happens before B then there must be a temporal relationship between them – one event can't occur without being preceded by some prior event happening.

seed42.I remember the day my father:
died. My mother, sister and I all lived in a one-bedroom apartment with our elderly parents.

My 87-year-old grandfather had been diagnosed three years before with heart disease that required constant medical attention. The stress of it all finally got to him; he was rushed into the hospital on Christmas Eve for an emergency coronary bypass surgery (CABG). He survived, but his prognosis wasn’t good: doctors told us there’s no way they can save his right leg.
That night we were forced to confront reality when we learned my beloved Grandpa would lose his left eye if doctors couldn't find some way to stop the bleeding from the broken blood vessels inside his eyes.

seed42.Money is:
a difficult subject. There are no hard and fast rules, but there are some general principles you can follow to avoid getting into financial trouble.
Save early
When it comes time for college, the first thing that should go out of your budget will be housing costs; rent or school fees don’t matter in this case at all. So try to save as much money as possible while you’re still young so you’ll have less stress later on when it’s time to take care of those bills.

Don't get attached

It's not wise to fall head over heels in love with any one piece of property – even if you've been living in it since before most grownups were born. Don't spend everything from your down payment until after you move
seed42.Death is:
a difficult topic for children to process. They may be confused about death and its meaning, or they might fear it will happen again. It's important that adults handle conversations with children in age-appropriate ways.
Children learn by observing the world around them - including each other. When you talk with your child about dying loved ones who have died, pay close attention to how you react as well as what you say. Children take their cues from grownups all the time; if there are signs of sadness, anger, confusion, etc., try not to hide these feelings – show vulnerability too so kids know this is okay material to discuss together.
It can also help to use books when talking with young children about losing someone very dear. Picture books provide comfort
